nodes.x: TO the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm of David. Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me [when I was] in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.
David reproveth and exhorteth his enemies, To, the chief Musician on Neginoth, A Psalm of David.
O ye sons of men, how long [will ye turn] my glory into shame? [how long] will ye love
But know that the LORD hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the LORD will hear when I call unto him.
Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah.
Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.

I testify to you that this is a day of sacrifice, that it is part of the Lord’s plan to bless us, His children. I pray we will do as the Psalmist exhorted—“offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put [our] trust in the Lord” (Psalm 4:5). In the name of Jesus Christ, amen. (Ezra Taft Benson, in Conference Report, Apr. 1979, 48; or Ensign, May 1979, 34)
[There be] many that say, Who will shew us [any] good? LORD, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.
Thou hast put gladness in my heart, more than in the time [that] their corn and their wine increased.
I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, LORD, only makest me dwell in safety.
